news.title: Sulphur shortage threatens Sh1.2 trillion cashewnut industry
news.body: A nationwide shortage of sulphur — a critical input used to control fungal disease and protect cashew yields — is threatening Tanzania's Sh1.2 trillion cashewnut industry ahead of the new growing season. Industry stakeholders have raised concerns that without timely sulphur dusting, crop quality and yields across the major cashew-growing regions of Mtwara, Lindi and Ruvuma could be significantly affected, with knock-on effects for farmer incomes and export volumes. Full report: thecitizen.co.tz

news.title: Tanzania's 2026/27 Agriculture Budget targets 32.4% surge in cash crop production and major avocado export push
news.body: Tanzania's 2026/27 Agriculture Budget, taking effect today, sets aggressive growth targets across the sector. The Ministry of Agriculture is aiming for a 32.4% increase in traditional cash crop production, targeting over 2.1 million tonnes. Avocado has been positioned as a major strategic export crop, with a production target of 235,000 tonnes and an export target of 40,000 tonnes, backed by distribution of over 1.7 million subsidised seedlings to farmers nationwide. The targets signal significant upcoming volume growth for transporters and new high-value export opportunities for investors and suppliers monitoring the supply chain.

news.title: USDA suspends countervailing duties on phosphate fertilizer imports, prices expected to fall 22%
news.body: The United States Department of Agriculture has announced a temporary suspension of countervailing duties on certain phosphate fertilizer imports, a policy shift expected to drop global phosphate fertilizer prices by approximately 22% and stabilise the international fertilizer supply chain ahead of upcoming planting seasons. Lower input costs are expected to ease pressure on production costs for Tanzanian farmers reliant on imported fertilizer, with knock-on effects for final market prices of produce in the coming season.
news.source: U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA) Official Press Release

news.title: Ministry of Agriculture seeks Environmental and Social Assessment for John Mwakangale Nanenane International Agricultural Trade Centre
news.body: A new high-value agricultural infrastructure tender has been published on the National e-Procurement System of Tanzania (NeST). The Ministry of Agriculture is seeking a qualified consultant to conduct an Environmental and Social Impact Assessment (ESIA) for the development of the John Mwakangale Nanenane International Agricultural Trade Centre. The tender represents a significant upcoming infrastructure investment in Tanzania's agricultural trade capacity, and is open to qualified contractors, engineers and environmental consultancy firms.
news.source: NeST — National e-Procurement System of Tanzania

news.title: Alternative fuels and AI-driven sorting reshape the agricultural equipment market
news.body: The global agricultural equipment market is shifting rapidly toward sustainability and automation. Major manufacturers are testing alternative fuel sources, including John Deere's ongoing trials of a new 8R Series tractor running entirely on E98 ethanol. At the same time, there is significant market growth in IoT-enabled precision equipment and AI-driven sorting systems, designed to address labour shortages and improve field efficiency for modern farm operators. The shift is expected to influence equipment leasing and logistics planning across the sector in the coming year.
news.source: Fortune Business Insights (June 2026 Market Report) & Glavpahar Agricultural Machinery News

news.title: TASAC reminds stakeholders to comply with Inland Clearance Depot Tariff Order GN. 459 of 2022
news.body: Tanzania Shipping Agencies Corporation (TASAC) has reminded all Port Terminal Operators, ICDs, Shipping Agents, Clearing and Forwarding Agents and Consignees to strictly comply with GN. No. 459 of 2022. Regulation 11 requires the party responsible for container transfer delays to bear associated storage costs. Regulation 19 requires Port Terminals or ICDs to compensate consignees for delays where the consignee was ready to take delivery. All regulated service providers must also maintain internal complaint handling mechanisms in line with GN. No. 338 of 2018.

news.title: TASAC prohibits demurrage charges on delayed empty container returns caused by ECD operators
news.body: TASAC has directed that shipping agents and related parties are strictly prohibited from charging demurrage or storage penalties on containers where delay in return is caused by refusal or inability of an Empty Container Depot to receive the container, congestion or capacity limitations at the ECD, or any factor beyond the control of the consignee or clearing agent. ECD operators are required to ensure timely receipt, operate within approved capacity, and coordinate with shipping agents. Shipping agents must provide alternative receiving arrangements where the designated ECD is unable to accept containers. This notice takes effect immediately upon issuance.

news.title: TPA reduces Port Infrastructure Development (PID) Levy from 9% to 4.5% effective 1 July 2026
news.body: Tanzania Private Sector Federation (TPSF) has announced that Tanzania Ports Authority (TPA) has officially reviewed and reduced the Port Infrastructure Development Levy from the initially proposed 9% to 4.5% of customs duty, effective 1st July 2026. This represents a 50% reduction following private sector consultations convened by TPSF on 17th March 2026. The levy aims to support port modernisation and expansion programmes estimated at TZS 16.1 trillion. The revised rate lowers the anticipated cost burden on importers by half while enabling TPA to mobilise resources for its infrastructure investment programme.

news.title: NSSF offers general amnesty to employers with outstanding statutory contribution arrears
news.body: The National Social Security Fund (NSSF) has announced a general amnesty to all employers with outstanding arrears of statutory contributions and penalties. Employers who remit all outstanding principal amounts on or before 31st August 2026 and remain current by 31st December 2026 will be relieved of 100% of overdue penalties. Those remitting by 31st October 2026 will receive 75% relief, and those remitting by 31st December 2026 receive 50% relief. Employers who are already compliant as of 31st May 2026 and remain current through 31st December 2026 also qualify for 100% penalty relief. The amnesty period runs from 1st June 2026 to 31st December 2026. For enquiries call toll-free: 0800116773.
news.source: NSSF — Public Relations and Member Education Unit

news.title: TBS requires Pre-Shipment Verification of Conformity for all regulated goods from China, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Mongolia and Macau
news.body: Tanzania Bureau of Standards (TBS) has reminded all importers of TBS-regulated goods from China Mainland, Hong Kong, Taiwan, Mongolia and Macau that all such goods must undergo Pre-Shipment Verification of Conformity to Standards (PVoC) in the country of export before shipment to Tanzania. TBS has contracted five authorised inspection agents: CCIC, Intertek International Limited, SGS, TUV Rheinland Middle East, and World Standardization Certification & Testing Group. Importers must liaise with these agents to obtain a Certificate of Conformity (CoC) before shipment. Failure to comply will result in inspection upon arrival and a penalty equal to 15% of the value of the goods. Visit www.tbs.go.tz or call 0800 110 827.
news.source: Tanzania Bureau of Standards (TBS) / Ministry of Industry and Trade
